revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:معبد الوادي]][[arz:معبد الوادى]][[ca:Temple de la Vall]][[cs:Údolní chrám]][[de:Taltempel]][[es:Templo del Valle]][[it:Tempio a valle]][[nl:Daltempel]][[pt:Templo do vale]][[sq:Tempujt në luginë të Egjiptit]] [[File:Sahure temp.jpg|thumb|Valley temple of {{ill|Sahure|en|Sahure|qid=Q243692|lt=Sahure|1=Sahure}}]]
A '''valley temple''' was an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=ancient Egyptian|1=Ancient Egypt}} temple building that formed part of a {{ill|pyramid complex|de|Pyramidenkomplex|ja|ピラミッド複合体|zh|金字塔建筑群|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was linked to a {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|qid=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple|1=Mortuary temple}} by a covered {{ill|Causeway (Ancient Egypt|de|Aufweg|it|Rampa processionale|cs|Vzestupná cesta|lt=causeway}}. From the reign of {{ill|Sneferu|en|Sneferu|qid=Q189371|lt=Sneferu|1=Sneferu}} through the end of the {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}, these temples were erected at the edge of the cultivable land, on the banks of canals.
Valley temples stood on a {{ill|Terrace (building)|en|Terrace (building)|qid=Q641406|lt=terrace (architecture)|1=Terrace (building)}} beside a canal and possessed a monumental {{ill|Wharf|en|Wharf|qid=Q828909|lt=quay|1=Wharf}} for the landing of large boats. They contained chapels for statues of the builder-king and roof sanctuaries reached by stairs. When Sneferu introduced the new type of pyramid precinct, the cultic encounter between king and gods was transferred from the pyramid enclosure to the valley. This probably continued the tradition of {{ill|god’s fortresses|de|Götterfestung|ja|神の要塞|zh|神堡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503324|1=god’s fortresses}} and {{ill|valley precincts|de|Talbezirk|ja|谷地区|zh|谷区|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q109715843|1=funerary enclosure}} of the {{ill|Thinite Period|de|Thinitenzeit|ja|ティニス時代|zh|蒂尼斯时期|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During ritual festivals the pyramid complex was visited by godsciteturn0 carrying their {{ill|barque (Egyptology)|de|Barke|ja|聖舟|zh|圣船|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and they were probably received by the king – or his images – in the valley temple.citeturn0 In later times aspects of the valley temple survived in {{ill|podium temples|de|Podiumstempel|ja|ポディウム神殿|zh|基座神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, built from the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} onwards opposite the {{ill|Pylon|en|Pylon|qid=Q2119636|lt=pylon (Egyptian architecture)|1=Pylon}} at canal or river bank. There too a cult image awaited the arrival of visiting deities. Interpretation as a royal {{ill|embalming house|de|Balsamierungshaus|ja|防腐処理所|zh|防腐处理所|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is no longer accepted.citeturn0
The valley temple of {{ill|Khafre|en|Khafre|qid=Q199861|lt=Khafre|1=Khafre}} is among the earliest completely preserved examples. Valley temples of {{ill|Menkaure|en|Menkaure|qid=Q200986|lt=Menkaure|1=Menkaure}}, Sahure, {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|en|Nyuserre Ini|qid=Q269200|lt=Nyuserre|1=Nyuserre Ini}}, {{ill|Unas|en|Unas|qid=Q244985|lt=Unas|1=Unas}}, {{ill|Pepi II Neferkare|en|Pepi II Neferkare|qid=Q6198|lt=Pepi II Neferkare|1=Pepi II Neferkare}}, {{ill|Senusret II|en|Senusret II|qid=Q18722|lt=Senusret II|1=Senusret II}} and {{ill|Amenemhat III|en|Amenemhat III|qid=Q19244|lt=Amenemhat III|1=Amenemhat III}} have also been excavated. Many more remain unexplored in the cultivated zone.citeturn0 During the Middle Kingdom comparable installations were built for private tombs at {{ill|Qaw el-Kebir|en|Qaw el-Kebir|qid=Q548283|lt=Qau el-Kebir|1=Qaw el-Kebir}}, {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|en|Thebes, Egypt|qid=Q101583|lt=Thebes, Egypt|1=Thebes, Egypt}} and {{ill|Aswan|en|Aswan|qid=Q29888|lt=Aswan|1=Aswan}}. The last example is beside the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut|en|Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut|qid=Q660692|lt=Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut|1=Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut}} at {{ill|Deir el-Bahari|en|Deir el-Bahari|qid=Q373909|lt=Deir el-Bahari|1=Deir el-Bahari}}.
== Literature == * {{ill|Dieter Arnold|en|Dieter Arnold|qid=Q104258|lt=Dieter Arnold|1=Dieter Arnold}}, ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Baukunst'', Düsseldorf 2000, p. 257. * V. A. Donohue, "Pr-nfr," *Journal of Egyptian Archaeology* 64 (1978), 143–148. * {{ill|I. E. S. Edwards|de|I. E. S. Edwards|ja|I. E. S. エドワーズ|zh|I·E·S·爱德华兹|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''The Pyramids of Egypt'', rev. ed. Harmondsworth 1985 (German trans. 1967), pp. 136–138. * Bernhard Grdseloff, ''Das ägyptische Reinigungszelt'', Cairo 1941. * {{ill|Frank Müller-Römer|de|Frank Müller-Römer|ja|フランク・ミュラー＝レーマー|zh|弗兰克·米ュラー-雷默|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Bau der Pyramiden im Alten Ägypten'', Maintal 2024, pp. 16 ff. * Paule Posener-Kriéger, ''Les archives du temple funéraire de Néferirkarê-Kakaï'', Cairo 1976, 549–563. * {{ill|Herbert Ricke|en|Herbert Ricke|qid=Q95436|lt=Herbert Ricke|1=Herbert Ricke}}, Siegfried Schott, ''Bemerkungen zur ägyptischen Baukunst des Alten Reiches'', Cairo 1950, 86–114. * {{ill|Rainer Stadelmann|en|Rainer Stadelmann|qid=Q88051|lt=Rainer Stadelmann|1=Rainer Stadelmann}}, "Taltempel," in: {{ill|Lexikon der Ägyptologie|de|Lexikon der Ägyptologie|ja|エジプト学事典|zh|埃及学词典|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, vol. 6 (1975–1992), 189–193.
